operating_state: Total revenues increased 13.8% to $96.4 billion driven by Google Search growing 11.7% and Google Cloud accelerating 31.7%. YouTube ads expanded while Google Network declined, with subscriptions and platforms growing 20.3%. Google Services operating income increased from revenue growth partially offset by legal settlement costs and traffic acquisition expenses. Google Cloud operating income surged primarily from revenue expansion offset by higher infrastructure and compensation costs.
cost_structure: Cost of revenues increased to $39.0 billion reflecting higher technical infrastructure investments and traffic acquisition costs rising to $14.7 billion. Research and development expenses rose 16.4% while general and administrative expenses increased significantly including legal settlement charges. TAC rate decreased to 20.6% from 20.7% due to revenue mix shift toward Google Search properties, with operating margin maintained at 32.4% despite higher absolute expense levels.
material_events: Alphabet entered definitive agreement to acquire Wiz cloud security platform for $32.0 billion in cash, expected to close in 2026 subject to regulatory approvals. Completed $28.6 billion in share repurchases with Board authorizing additional $70.0 billion repurchase program. Issued debt totaling $5.0 billion in U.S. dollars and €6.75 billion in euros across multiple maturities. Increased quarterly dividend 5% to $0.21 per share.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency translation adjustments generated $3.3 billion positive impact primarily benefiting from euro strengthening with 52% of revenues generated outside the United States. Effective tax rate increased from 15.4% to 17.1% primarily due to decreased stock-based compensation tax benefits and non-deductible legal settlement. New U.S. federal tax law changes signed July 2025 may affect consolidated financial statements beginning Q3 2025.
forward_outlook: Revenue backlog reached $108.2 billion with approximately 55% expected to be recognized over next 24 months, primarily from Google Cloud commitments. Capital expenditures accelerated to $39.6 billion in first six months reflecting continued investment in technical infrastructure to support AI products and services. Management expects Wiz acquisition to integrate into Google Cloud segment upon closing in 2026.
uncertainty_profile: Antitrust remedy proceedings present material business model risks with DOJ decisions expected August 2025 for search matters and September 2025 for advertising technology, with potential structural changes to search and advertising businesses. European Commission fines totaled €4.1 billion under appeal while privacy settlement agreements remain subject to final court approval. Long-term income taxes payable of $10.0 billion primarily relate to uncertain tax positions with ongoing regulatory investigations across multiple jurisdictions.
